rulings.Case Title: RAMESES JOHNSON VS. DARREN GATES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Order Of Involuntary Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 17, 2025, line 1. PLAINTIFF RAMESES JOHNSON'S Motion For Relief From Order Of Involuntary Dismissal. Plaintiff Rameses Johnson's motion for relief from order of involuntary dismissal is denied. Johnson's case was dismissed as a sanction pursuant to Government Code Section 68608(b) for his failure to show cause why the matter should not be dismissed after he failed to serve the defendant, appear at case management conferences, or file written responses to the court's orders to show cause issued over a lengthy period. The complaint was filed on January 4, 2022. The case was dismissed on August 20, 2024, at which point summons had not been served. Johnson files his motion under CCP Section 473(b) and provides a declaration attesting to the reasons why he did not prosecute his case. The declaration does not specifically address the circumstances at the August 20, 2024 show cause hearing or why he missed that appearance. The August 20 order found no good cause or substantial justification to excuse Plaintiff's failure to comply with prior orders, and that a less severe sanction would be ineffective due to the history of lack of compliance. The court does not find that Plaintiff's declaration submitted with his moving papers establishes excusable neglect in light of the many opportunities that the court gave to plaintiff to comply with the court's orders to advance his case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CHAK LUNG SIU VS. MING LIAN ZHOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 17, 2025, line 3. DEFENDANT HUI DA CONSTRUCTION INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Cross-Complaint. Defendant/Cross Complainant HUI DA Construction Inc, DBA HD Construction's motion for leave to file a First Amended Cross Complaint ("FACC") is GRANTED. Defendant/Cross Complainant shall file and serve the FACC by April 1, 2025. The motion substantially complies with CRC 3.1324. "Great liberality is indulged in matters of amendment to the end that lawsuits may be determined upon their merits." (Desny v. Wilder (1956) 46 Cal.2d 715, 751.) The proposed amendments do not change the tenor of the case and Plaintiff/Cross Defendant Chak Lung Siu has not demonstrated cognizable prejudice. The trial date is set for October 6, 2025, more than six months from now. Plaintiff/Cross Defendant argues that the amended or new claims fail as a matter of law, but the parties can test the viability of the amended or new claims after amendment on noticed motions in which the issues are squared. (California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 [preferable practice is to grant leave to amend and allow the parties to subsequently test the viability of the claim] (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: HAIQING QIU VS. EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT STATE OF
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for March 17, 2025, line 4. DEFENDANT EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Hiaqing Qiu filed a First Amended Complaint (FAC) alleging that he should have received certain COVID-related benefits from defendant Employment Development Department, including unemployment benefits and rental assistance. The complaint also contains exhibits showing Qiu's applications for these benefits, his supporting documentation, and his appeals. Defendant EDD now demurs to the FAC in its entirety. Plaintiff opposes (in an overlength filing. See Rule of Court 3.1113(d). The court admonishes Plaintiff to adhere to the page limits of the rule in the future; if Plaintiff does not, the court may strike Plaintiff's brief or strike all pages past the limit.) The court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend. EDD is liable only as provided by statute. (Gov. Code 815(a).) Qiu identifies no statute under which he can recover. Moreover, the Government Tort Claims Act requires a claimant to present a government claim before suing for money or damages. (Gov. Code 810.) Plaintiff does not plead that he submitted a government claim to EDD or that he exhausted his administrative remedies prior to bringing suit. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend only as follows. If he can amend in good faith to plead his compliance with the claims presentation and exhaustion requirements before bringing suit, he has leave to do so. His amendment must also cure the deficiency that he does not identify a statutory basis permitting him to recover damages from EDD. Plaintiff has 30 days from entry of this order to amend. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
